# Tractatus Logico-Philosophicus

*[Wittgenstein](../philosophers/wittgenstein.md) · 1921*

A brief treatise on logic and language that makes philosophy not a doctrine but an activity of clarification: it draws the limits of what can be said in order to dispel the confusions that breed metaphysical problems. It closes on the famous line: 'Whereof one cannot speak, thereof one must be silent.'
